question: What tests and implementation contracts should prove every frontend environment defaults to https://life.dock108.dev while preserving only explicit launch-arg local overrides?
Which app code, settings copy, fixtures, exports, and tests can expose provider-secret language or raw service tokens, and what guardrails currently constrain them?
How does the chat send flow behave across local save, extraction success, backend failure, token failure, retry scheduling, stale completion, and web request modes?
What identity keys and source-record invariants prevent duplicate events, rules, and notes during retry or repeated extraction?
Do backend error response bodies and iOS decoding/error mapping have a stable contract for auth, timeout, rate-limit, server, validation, and malformed-response cases?
Which SwiftData schema, migration, fixture, and generated-heavy files are app code versus deliberate iOS coverage exclusions or separate reports?
How should xccov JSON from LifeOrganizeTests.xcresult identify only the LifeOrganize app target, exclude test targets, and report any excluded files visibly?
What simulator/device/OS pin is compatible with the repo scripts and current GitHub macOS runners, and what changes would be required if moving away from iPhone 16 iOS 18.6?
What iOS workflow triggers, jobs, signing flags, artifact paths, and no-deploy safeguards should match the current Xcode project and braindump status-check shape?
Which UI and unit tests currently use deterministic extraction versus real backend clients, and where should CI prevent routine live OpenAI calls while still proving production backend defaults?
Should screenshot comparison run on every frontend PR or only UI-touching changes, and which result bundles, actuals, and diffs should be uploaded on failure?
How should backend, iOS test/coverage, screenshot, Docker smoke, and production smoke commands compose into verify-backend, verify-ios, and verify-all scripts with first-failure exits?
What are the tradeoffs of keeping backend CI and Docker on Python 3.14 versus pinning CI/runtime to a stable 3.12 or 3.13 while retaining requires-python >=3.11?
How should pytest-cov be added and configured to gate Backend app and main at 80% while excluding Alembic boilerplate deliberately?
Which FastAPI route-level tests are needed for missing, malformed, valid, and admin-auth tokens across extraction, web request, usage, and logs endpoints?
How does the current per-device rate limiter count requests and reset windows, and what tests should cover endpoint separation, retry headers, shared IP assumptions, and token redaction?
How does OpenAIGateway map provider auth, rate-limit, timeout, network, server, malformed-body, and invalid-response cases, and what should be tested without live OpenAI calls?
Where can DTO/schema drift occur between iOS BackendAIRequest.swift and backend Pydantic schemas/OpenAI schema names, and what parity tests or fixtures could detect it?
What behavior do security headers and request-size middleware currently guarantee, and how do they behave for OPTIONS, missing Content-Length, invalid Content-Length, and oversized bodies?
What admin log fields, SSE shape, session-cookie behavior, redaction guarantees, and retention limits are load-bearing for the backend log surface?
How should tests or smoke commands prove Alembic upgrades an empty database, migrations are idempotent, request metadata persists, and database failures are handled?
What does the current Docker Compose health/migration flow verify, and where should CI add image build, container start, /healthz, and migration-once/idempotency checks?
What assumptions does update_caddy_site_block.py make about Caddyfile parsing, site header matching, sudo deployment, and rollback/manual deploy flows?
Should production smoke remain unauthenticated /healthz or add a private admin smoke endpoint, and how would that interact with deploy secrets and Caddy routing?
How should the existing backend workflow jobs map to desired status checks for tests, lint, compile, coverage, docker build, deploy health, and prod health smoke?
What current rollback workflow assumptions exist around image tags, migrations, schema compatibility, and whether rollback should run migrations automatically?
Which repo docs are stale or incomplete relative to current workflows, backend CI/CD, local gates, coverage, and screenshot tooling?
What exact GitHub job names and future workflow check names should branch protection use for backend, iOS, screenshot, coverage, Docker, and production smoke gates?
